mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near historic lows and continuing a multi-month downward or flat trajectory signals monetary accommodation, which lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lifts S&P 500 prices over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or accommodative periods show no statistically significant positive mean return versus tightening periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a 6.5% threshold, it signals labor-market deterioration consistent with recessionary conditions, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: In triggered instances (UNRATE > 6.5%), forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not significantly lower (p > 0.05) than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS and INDPRO confirms a genuine broad-based real-economy expansion (labor demand plus factory output), which jointly signals sustainable earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone carries the same confirmatory power.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS+INDPRO positive-growth signal produces forward 20d returns no better than either single-series signal alone (interaction term contributes < 0.01 incremental Rank IC), or joint-positive months show negative mean returns in more than 40% of walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (rising FEDFUNDS), a decline in HOUST (housing starts) signals rate-sensitive demand destruction that leads broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; in easing or flat-rate regimes, HOUST declines carry no such predictive power because housing weakness is not compounded by tightening credit conditions.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is rising
failure_condition: During periods when both trigger conditions hold, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the HOUST-return relationship is statistically indistinguishable (p > 0.05) between tightening and easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) reflects strengthening consumer demand that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 419027 million as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward 5d SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO year-over-year growth decelerates and crosses below zero, it signals an industrial contraction that foreshadows broader earnings weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of negative INDPRO YoY growth are followed by positive forward 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where GDPC1 growth remains positive while RSAFS growth simultaneously stalls or declines signals an unsustainable growth composition (output not matched by consumer spending), which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the divergence resolves through demand-side correction.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence signal fails to outperform a naive GDPC1-only trend model in Rank IC by more than 0.02 over any 3-year walk-forward window, or divergence periods show non-negative mean forward 60d returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month deceleration in PAYEMS growth below a defined threshold signals an abrupt labor-demand shock that triggers short-term risk-off equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 0 (net job losses)
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135474 thousand as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Instances of net monthly job losses are followed by positive forward 5d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) versus baseline.
